headline: The gold medal for deception: How a professor created an international award out of thin air
description: In 2016, Florent Montaclair stepped into the spotlight when he received the Gold Medal of Philology, a sort of Nobel Prize for the discipline. In fact, the award was created by Montaclair himself. It took an investigation by Romanian journalists to uncover this hoax, which is now the subject of a judicial inquiry in France.

Elon Musk summoned by French prosecutors after 15-month probe
In Milan, Europe's far right gathers amid shifting era and enduring identity politics
description: Just days after Viktor Orban's defeat in Hungary, Matteo Salvini, Italy's deputy prime minister, hosted a far-right rally under the banner 'Without Fear: In Europe We Are Masters in Our Own House.'

headline: Bulgarian legislative elections: Corruption and ties to Russia at the heart of the campaign
description: Leading in the polls, former president Rumen Radev, known for his ambiguous stance on Russia, has made headlines for his anti-corruption platform and promises to 'topple the oligarchy.' Sunday's vote marks the eighth election in five years.

headline: Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
description: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

headline: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
description: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse

description: Following a visit by President John Dramani Mahama to the Elysée, Ghana's foreign affairs minister announced on April 8 that Paris would be willing to 'collaborate' on 'restorative justice.' Those close to Emmanuel Macron confirmed that the French president supported the initiative.

headline: Rubirosa's celebration of noble chic in the heart of Paris
description: Designer Lauren Rubinski has created her small Parisian boutique as an old-fashioned jewel box. Her refined, unisex range, where shirts take center stage, has attracted a clientele who value quality above all else.

headline: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
description: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.
